workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and language MUST be tailored to {user_skill_level}

This workflow orchestrates dev-story, qa-generate-e2e-tests, and code-review as sub-workflows. When invoking a sub-workflow, load its instructions file and execute EVERY step to completion, then return control here.

After Step 2 (preflight approval), the loop MUST run autonomously through Steps 3-4. Do NOT ask the user any further question until Step 5 (report) or Step 6 (PR opened), UNLESS a HALT condition triggers because an operation outside the pre-approved batch is required.
Human gates are exactly two: story-ready (Step 2) and merge-ready (post-Step 6). Everything between is autonomous.

step.goal: Determine input source and produce a dev-ready story

check.ask: Provide the full path to the story file:
check.action: Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}
Read COMPLETE story file to confirm it exists and has a Status of ready-for-dev

check.ask: Story status is not "ready-for-dev". Continue anyway? [y/n]:

check.action: HALT — user aborted

check.ask: Provide the Jira issue key (e.g. TRAIN-42):
check.action: Call MCP tool `mcp__ELCA-MCP__elca_jira_get_issue` with the key to fetch summary, description, acceptance criteria
Load and execute {{create_story_workflow}} with the Jira context as input to produce a story file
Store the resulting story path as {{story_path}}

check.ask: Describe the feature (single paragraph or bullet list):
check.action: Load and execute {{create_story_workflow}} with the user's description as input to produce a story file
Store the resulting story path as {{story_path}}
Read {{sprint_status}} and find the first story with status `ready-for-dev`
Store that story's file path as {{story_path}}

check.output: No ready-for-dev story in {{sprint_status}}. Choose option 1, 2, or 3 instead.

step.action: Confirm {{story_path}} is set; abort with an error message if not

step.goal: Enumerate and collect a single preflight approval batch
step.critical: This is the ONLY human gate inside the loop. After the user approves the batch, Steps 3-4 run autonomously.
step.action:
Inspect {{story_path}} and the project structure to predict which actions the loop will need. Build the approval list in TWO layers:
**Layer A — Command categories (default view):** - Run test / build commands (mvn, npm, gradle, pytest — whatever the project uses) - Read/Write/Edit files under the project source and test trees - Run `git` commands (add, commit, push -u origin HEAD) - Run `gh pr create --base {{target_branch}}` to open the PR - Call MCP tools required for the story (e.g. Jira for context, if relevant)
**Layer B — Exact commands with args (drill-down):** For each category, list the concrete commands you expect to run based on the story. Example: - `.\mvnw test` (or `.\mvnw verify` for the full build) - `git push -u origin feature/STORY-1-8-admin-delete-menu-item` - `gh pr create --base main --title "…" --body-file .pr-body.md` - Files that will be created or modified (predicted from story tasks)

check.ask: Now choose [a] approve, [m] modify, or [c] cancel:
Which categories should be excluded? (comma-separated numbers or names)
check.action: Remove excluded categories from the approval set. Note: if the loop later needs an excluded operation, it will HALT and ask.
check.ask: Confirm reduced approval set [a] or cancel [c]:

check.action: HALT
Store the approved category set as {{approved_actions}} for the rest of the run
Set {{iteration}} = 0
Set {{prior_failures}} = "" (empty context; will accumulate across iterations)

step.critical: Do NOT prompt the user during this step. If an operation outside {{approved_actions}} is needed, HALT with a clear message stating what extra approval is required and resume-from-here instructions.
step.action: Load and execute {{dev_story_workflow}} to completion with {{story_path}} as input. If {{prior_failures}} is non-empty, prepend it as context so the dev agent addresses the previous iteration's failures.
Load and execute {{qa_e2e_workflow}} to completion, targeting the code changes from step 3a
Run the generated + existing test suite (use the project's test command; typically `mvn verify` for Java, `npm test` for JS/TS, `pytest` for Python)

check.action: Append test failure summary to {{prior_failures}}, tagged as "Iteration {{iteration}} test failures:"
check.output: [qa] {{iteration}}: FAILED — {{test_failure_count}} failures. Feeding back to dev.

step.action: Load and execute {{code_review_workflow}} to completion on the changes from step 3a
Capture review findings as {{review_findings}}, categorized as `blocker`, `major`, `minor`, `nit`

check.action: Set {{loop_result}} = "clean"
Append review findings to {{prior_failures}}, tagged as "Iteration {{iteration}} review findings:"
check.output: [review] {{iteration}}: {{review_finding_count}} findings ({{blocker_count}} blocker, {{major_count}} major, {{minor_count}} minor, {{nit_count}} nit). Feeding back to dev.

Story Loop — HALT: {max_iterations} iterations reached without convergence.
Accumulated feedback across iterations:
{{prior_failures}}
Recommended next steps: 1. Review the current branch's state (`git status`, `git diff`) 2. Address the residual findings manually 3. Re-run this workflow with the same story, or `bmad-bmm-correct-course` if the story itself needs revision
check.action: HALT — user intervention required

step.goal: Ship: push branch and open PR
step.action: Determine current branch name via `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`
Compose PR title from the story title/key
Compose PR body containing: - Link to the story file - Summary of changes (from Dev Agent Record in the story) - Test summary from qa-generate-e2e-tests - Code review outcome - Iteration count
Capture the PR URL from the gh output

check.if: {{sprint_status}} file exists and story has a matching key
check.action: Update the story's status in {{sprint_status}} to `ready-for-review`
